result:
Build is green (0 errors).

## Report

**Root cause.** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/auxlib.rs:1050` — `load_filex` was a Phase-A stub that returned `Err(LuaError::runtime("...not implemented..."))`. C-Lua's `luaL_loadfilex` returns a status code with an error string left on the stack on file errors, and `load_aux` (base.rs:191) converts that to `(nil, errmsg)`. Returning `Err` instead bubbled past `load_aux` through `loadfile_fn`, so `loadfile(removed_file)` raised at line 76 of `files.lua` (`assert(not loadfile(file))`) instead of returning nil.

**Fix.** Implemented `load_filex` for real in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/auxlib.rs`: slurp the file via `std::fs::read`, strip an optional UTF-8 BOM and shebang line, feed the bytes into `lua_vm::api::load` with chunkname `@<filename>`. On open failure it pushes `"cannot open <filename>: <io-err>"` and returns `LUA_ERRFILE` so `load_aux` produces `(nil, errmsg)`. The `forbidden-import.sh` Stop hook does not ban `std::fs` (only tokio/async/futures/rayon/std::process::Command-outside-lua-cli), and the original TODO explicitly named this as the Phase-B resolution. Trailer todos count updated 19 → 10.

**New state.** I did not re-execute the test (per instructions). The previous failure point (`luaL_loadfilex not implemented`) cannot fire any more — that error string was deleted. `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean (0 errors).

tool_input.command: cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^error" | head -5; echo "exit=$?"
cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^error" | head -5 ; printf '\nbuild=%s\n' "$?"
cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>/tmp/build_out.txt; echo "exit=$?"; head -5 /tmp/build_out.txt | head
